ucs.title: [LLM] CURIUM/TA456 long-grooming spearphishing → Office-spawned scripting host within minutes of first-contact email
ucs.description: Hunts CURIUM's signature tradecraft: months-long social-engineering rapport (e.g. the 'Marcella Flores' persona used by TA456 against aerospace contractors) culminating in a single weaponised attachment (LEMPO/Liderbot dropper) that the target opens, causing Outlook/Office to spawn a scripting host. Joins inbound mail from low-history external senders to subsequent script-host execution on the recipient's device within 10 minutes — the exact chain Facebook/Mandiant attributed to TA456 / Tortoiseshell.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as emailTime values(All_Email.subject) as subject values(All_Email.src_user) as sender from datamodel=Email where All_Email.action=delivered All_Email.direction=inbound All_Email.file_name=* by All_Email.message_id All_Email.recipient All_Email.src_user | rename All_Email.* as * | eventstats count as senderHistory by sender | where senderHistory<=3 | rename recipient as user | join type=inner user [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as procTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.process_name) as child values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.dest) as host from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","explorer.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe") by Processes.user Processes.dest Processes.process_id | rename Processes.user as user] | where procTime>=emailTime AND procTime<=emailTime+600 | eval delaySec=procTime-emailTime | table emailTime procTime delaySec user host sender subject parent child cmd
ucs.kql:
// CURIUM/TA456 social-engineering payload chain — first-contact sender + Office-spawned scripting host within 10 minutes
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let HistoryWindow = 90d;
let WindowSec = 600;
let ScriptingHosts = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","wget.exe"]);
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let SenderHistory = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(HistoryWindow) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound"
    | summarize PriorMsgs = count() by SenderFromAddress;
let SuspectEmails = EmailEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EmailDirection == "Inbound" and DeliveryAction == "Delivered"
    | where AttachmentCount > 0
    | join kind=inner (
        EmailAttachmentInfo
        | where FileType in~ ("zip","rar","iso","img","lnk","exe","scr","doc","docm","xlsm","htm","html","hta","chm")
        | project NetworkMessageId, AttachFile = FileName, FileType, AttachSHA256 = SHA256
      ) on NetworkMessageId
    | join kind=leftouter SenderHistory on SenderFromAddress
    | where isnull(PriorMsgs) or PriorMsgs <= 2          // CURIUM uses fresh / never-before-seen aliases
    | project NetworkMessageId, EmailTime = Timestamp, SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain,
              RecipientEmailAddress, Subject, AttachFile, FileType, AttachSHA256;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
| where FileName in~ (ScriptingHosts)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("whoami","systeminfo","net user","-EncodedCommand","DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-WebRequest")
| join kind=inner SuspectEmails on $left.AccountUpn == $right.RecipientEmailAddress
| where Timestamp between (EmailTime .. EmailTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project Timestamp, EmailTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, EmailTime),
          DeviceName, AccountUpn,
          SenderFromAddress, SenderFromDomain, Subject, AttachFile, AttachSHA256,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          Child  = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// CURIUM/TA456 social-engineering payload chain — Sentinel port using OfficeActivity + SecurityEvent 4688
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let HistoryWindow = 90d;
let WindowSec = 600;
let ScriptingHosts = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe"]);
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","explorer.exe"]);
let SenderHistory = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(HistoryWindow) .. ago(LookbackDays))
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange" and Operation =~ "MailItemsAccessed"
    | summarize PriorMsgs = count() by Sender = tostring(Sender);
let SuspectEmails = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
    | where isnotempty(ItemAttachments)
    | extend Sender = tostring(Sender)
    | join kind=leftouter SenderHistory on Sender
    | where isnull(PriorMsgs) or PriorMsgs <= 2
    | project EmailTime = TimeGenerated, Sender, Recipient = MailboxOwnerUPN, Subject, ItemAttachments;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (OfficeParents)
| where ChildLeaf in (ScriptingHosts)
   or CommandLine has_any ("whoami","systeminfo","net user","-EncodedCommand","DownloadString","IEX","Invoke-WebRequest")
| extend UPN = tolower(tostring(strcat(SubjectUserName,"@",SubjectDomainName)))
| join kind=inner SuspectEmails on $left.UPN == $right.Recipient
| where TimeGenerated between (EmailTime .. EmailTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project TimeGenerated, EmailTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, EmailTime),
          Computer, Account, Sender, Subject,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: CURIUM/TA456's defining tradecraft is patient, persona-driven social engineering (Marcella Flores, fake recruiters on LinkedIn/Facebook) — when the payload finally lands, it almost always comes from a sender the target has only recently engaged with, never from established business-correspondence partners. Pairing 'sender with ≤2 prior messages in 90 days' with 'Office process spawned a scripting

ucs.title: [LLM] Tortoiseshell (CURIUM) IIS web-shell on compromised IT service provider — w3wp.exe spawning recon commands
ucs.description: Hunts CURIUM/Tortoiseshell's signature post-exploitation step against MSP/IT-service-provider OWA/IIS servers (Symantec 2019 reporting): after deploying Backdoor.Syskit (`syskit.exe`) and supporting web shells, the operator drives the shell to run hands-on-keyboard discovery — `whoami`, `systeminfo`, `net user /domain`, `nltest /domain_trusts`, `tasklist`, `ipconfig /all` — all spawned by `w3wp.exe`. This chain is the cluster's primary route to downstream lateral movement at the IT provider before pivoting into customer tenants.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(Processes.process) as cmds values(Processes.process_name) as children values(Processes.user) as users from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","arp.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*whoami*","*systeminfo*","*net user*","*net group*","*nltest*","*tasklist*","*ipconfig /all*","*query user*","*net localgroup administrators*")) by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process Processes.parent_process_id | rename Processes.* as * | where count >= 2 | sort - lastSeen
ucs.kql:
// Tortoiseshell/CURIUM IIS web-shell hands-on-keyboard recon (Backdoor.Syskit pattern)
let ReconBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","arp.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe"]);
let ReconCmds = dynamic(["whoami","systeminfo","net user","net group","net localgroup administrators","nltest /domain_trusts","nltest /dclist","tasklist","ipconfig /all","query user","net view","arp -a","route print"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FileName in~ (ReconBins)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any (ReconCmds)
| extend AppPool = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| summarize ReconHits = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen  = max(Timestamp),
            DistinctChildren = dcount(FileName),
            Children  = make_set(FileName, 32),
            SampleCmds = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 16),
            User      = any(AccountName)
            by DeviceName, AppPool
| where ReconHits >= 2 and DistinctChildren >= 2          // 1 hit = noise; chained recon = web-shell driving
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Tortoiseshell/CURIUM IIS web-shell recon — Sentinel port (SecurityEvent 4688)
let ReconBins = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ipconfig.exe","nltest.exe","quser.exe","query.exe","arp.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","sc.exe","wmic.exe"]);
let ReconCmds = dynamic(["whoami","systeminfo","net user","net group","net localgroup administrators","nltest /domain_trusts","nltest /dclist","tasklist","ipconfig /all","query user","net view","arp -a"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
         ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf == "w3wp.exe"
| where ChildLeaf in (ReconBins)
   or CommandLine has_any (ReconCmds)
| summarize ReconHits = count(),
            FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen  = max(TimeGenerated),
            DistinctChildren = dcount(ChildLeaf),
            Children = make_set(ChildLeaf, 32),
            SampleCmds = make_set(CommandLine, 16),
            Account  = any(Account)
            by Computer, ParentProcessName
| where ReconHits >= 2 and DistinctChildren >= 2
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: IIS Worker Process Spawning Recon Commands (CURIUM / Tortoiseshell)
id: 7e2d1a90-c4b7-4f3e-9c1a-curium-webshell-recon
status: experimental
description: Detects w3wp.exe spawning native Windows discovery utilities consistent with Tortoiseshell/CURIUM Backdoor.Syskit web-shell hands-on-keyboard activity against compromised IT-service-provider OWA/IIS servers.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1012/
  - https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/blogs/threat-intelligence/tortoiseshell-apt-supply-chain
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  child_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\quser.exe'
      - '\query.exe'
      - '\arp.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\reg.exe'
      - '\sc.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
  child_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'whoami'
      - 'systeminfo'
      - 'net user'
      - 'net group'
      - 'net localgroup administrators'
      - 'nltest /domain_trusts'
      - 'tasklist'
      - 'ipconfig /all'
      - 'query user'
      - 'net view'
  condition: parent and (child_image or child_cmd)
fields:
  - Computer
  - User
  - ParentImage
  - Image
  - CommandLine
falsepositives:
  - Custom IIS-hosted apps that legitimately shell out (rare; baseline per app-pool and exclude)
  - SCOM / monitoring agents executing under the IIS identity
level: high
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.discovery
  - attack.t1082
  - attack.t1124
  - apt.curium
  - apt.tortoiseshell
ucs.rationale: CURIUM (Symantec's Tortoiseshell, Microsoft's Crimson Sandstorm) was first publicly reported targeting Saudi IT service providers via web shells dropped onto OWA / IIS servers, with Backdoor.Syskit and supporting shells running predictable Windows-native recon. w3wp.exe spawning cmd/powershell/net/whoami/nltest is essentially never legitimate on a healthy IIS box, and chaining ≥2 distinct recon bi
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
